element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns basic movement and maze navigation in enclosed safe space before ghosts become threatening
Primary objective introduced immediately; player learns collection mechanic in low-threat environment

element_introduction_order.element: Four colored ghosts (Blinky, Pinky, Inky, Clyde)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Ghosts introduced from start with distinct AI personalities; player learns evasion and ghost behavior patterns across levels
Located at four corners of maze; player learns strategic collection and ghost vulnerability mechanics
Appear after eating certain dot count; teaches secondary scoring objective and timing awareness
Located on maze sides; player discovers tunnel mechanics and ghost slowdown effect in tunnels

typical_relief_triggers: Eating power pellet causes ghosts to turn blue and reverse direction
Eating a blue ghost grants bonus points and removes immediate threat temporarily
Using warp tunnels to escape pursuit (ghosts move slower in tunnels)
Reaching level completion (all dots eaten) triggers inter-level cutscene
typical_tension_triggers: Ghost proximity increases threat level during dot collection
Power pellet effect duration decreases with each level, reducing relief window
Ghosts increase speed progressively across levels
Multiple ghosts coordinating pursuit (Pinky and Inky position ahead, Blinky gives direct chase)
Being cornered in maze dead-ends with approaching ghosts
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Rhythmic tension-relief cycles driven by power pellet availability; initial levels emphasize learning with manageable threat escalation; mid-to-late levels shift to sustained high tension as power pellet duration shrinks and ghost speed increases

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus fruit items appear in center area after eating threshold number of dots; players may choose to collect for points or avoid if threatened
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Warp tunnels on maze sides provide escape routes; ghosts move slower in tunnels creating strategic safe passage opportunities

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory threat level with slower ghosts and longer power pellet duration; player learns all core mechanics (movement, collection, evasion, power pellet usage) with relatively forgiving chase patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Ghost speed increases; power pellet duration shortens noticeably; bonus items introduce secondary objective; players must balance risk-reward of collection vs. evasion
difficulty_curve.late_game: Ghosts move significantly faster; power pellet effect duration becomes very brief; energizers eventually disappear entirely; sustained high-threat environment requiring expert navigation and ghost AI prediction
notable_spikes: Power pellet duration decrease creates difficulty jumps at regular progression intervals
Transition to levels without power pellet effect represents major difficulty spike
Level 256 (kill screen) features integer overflow making level uncompletable

notable_level_patterns.description: Player navigates enclosed maze eating dots while avoiding four ghosts with distinct AI behaviors; power pellets provide temporary vulnerability window for ghost consumption; completion triggers level advancement and cutscene
Between level completions, short humorous cutscenes featuring Pac-Man and Blinky provide pacing relief and narrative flavor while allowing player brief rest before difficulty increase
Systematic increase in ghost speed and decrease in power pellet duration across consecutive levels; creates graduated challenge curve rather than dramatic spikes (except at kill screen)
